# WebMind AI

> WebMind is an open-source platform for running AI models — large language models (LLMs) and specialized ML models — directly in the web browser using the WebGPU API. No server, no cloud, no API key required. All computation happens locally on the user's device. Built by Alican Atamer.

## What WebMind Does

WebMind provides a browser-based interface for two AI runtimes:

- **WebLLM** (by MLC AI): Enables large language model chat and reasoning in Chrome/Edge using WebGPU. Users download a model once to the browser cache, then run inference entirely client-side.
- **Transformers.js** (by Hugging Face): Enables specialized AI tasks — NLP, computer vision, audio, and multimodal — in the browser via ONNX Runtime (WebAssembly or WebGPU).

Your data never leaves your device. There are no API calls to external servers during inference. Pageview analytics (Vercel Analytics) are the only external data transmission — inference inputs and outputs are never sent anywhere.

## Supported LLM Models (WebLLM)

| Model | Provider | Parameters | Size | VRAM Required | Quantization | License | Best For |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Llama 3.2 1B Instruct | Meta | 1B | 800 MB | 1 GB | q4f32 | Llama 3.2 Community | Quick Q&A, low-end devices |
| Llama 3.2 3B Instruct | Meta | 3B | 1.9 GB | 2 GB | q4f32 | Llama 3.2 Community | Conversational AI, translation |
| Phi-3.5 Mini Instruct | Microsoft | 3.8B | 2.2 GB | 2.5 GB | q4f16 | MIT | Math, STEM, reasoning |
| Gemma 2 2B IT | Google | 2B | 1.5 GB | 2 GB | q4f32 | Gemma Terms | Multilingual chat, writing |
| Qwen 2.5 1.5B Instruct | Alibaba | 1.5B | 950 MB | 1 GB | q4f32 | Apache 2.0 | Multilingual apps, Asian languages |
| Qwen 2.5 7B Instruct | Alibaba | 7B | 4.5 GB | 6 GB | q4f16 | Apache 2.0 | Complex reasoning, 100+ languages |
| Mistral 7B Instruct v0.3 | Mistral AI | 7B | 4.3 GB | 5 GB | q4f16 | Apache 2.0 | General chat, coding |
| SmolLM2 1.7B Instruct | Hugging Face | 1.7B | 1.1 GB | 1.5 GB | q4f16 | Apache 2.0 | Browser-optimized, mobile AI |
| DeepSeek R1 Distill 1.5B | DeepSeek | 1.5B | 1.0 GB | 1 GB | q4f32 | MIT | Math, logic, chain-of-thought |

## Supported AI Tasks (Transformers.js)

### NLP / Text Tasks
- **Sentiment Analysis** — DistilBERT (67 MB, CPU compatible) — positive/negative/neutral text classification
- **Named Entity Recognition** — BERT NER (420 MB, CPU compatible) — extracts people, places, organizations from text
- **Translation (200 languages)** — NLLB-200 Meta (1.2 GB, CPU compatible) — including Turkish, Kurdish, rare languages
- **Text Summarization** — DistilBART (700 MB, CPU compatible) — condenses long documents
- **Question Answering** — DistilBERT QA (260 MB, CPU compatible) — extracts answers from context
- **Zero-Shot Classification** — BART (1.6 GB) — custom category labels without training
- **Text Generation** — LaMini (940 MB) — instruction-following rewriting and paraphrasing

### Vision / Image Tasks
- **Image Classification** — ViT Google (330 MB) — 1000 ImageNet categories
- **Object Detection** — DETR Meta (160 MB) — locates and identifies objects with bounding boxes
- **Depth Estimation** — Depth Anything ByteDance (100 MB) — creates depth maps from 2D images

### Audio Tasks
- **Speech Recognition** — Whisper Tiny OpenAI (150 MB, CPU compatible) — transcribes audio in 99 languages

### Multimodal Tasks
- **Image-Text Matching** — CLIP OpenAI (600 MB) — matches images to text descriptions with custom labels

## Technical Details

### Browser Requirements
- **WebLLM (LLMs)**: Chrome 113+ or Edge 113+ with WebGPU enabled
- **Transformers.js**: All modern browsers (WebAssembly fallback works on Firefox and Safari)
- **SharedArrayBuffer**: Required for multi-threaded WebLLM inference (enabled on HTTPS)

### Architecture
- Next.js 16 App Router (TypeScript)
- WebGPU API for GPU-accelerated inference
- WebAssembly (ONNX Runtime Web) for CPU fallback
- Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin (required for SharedArrayBuffer)
- Models cached in browser Cache Storage after first download

### Performance Characteristics
- Small models (1B, ~800 MB): Load in 30–60 seconds on broadband; inference at 15–40 tokens/sec
- Medium models (3B, ~2 GB): Load in 1–3 minutes; inference at 8–20 tokens/sec
- Large models (7B, ~4.5 GB): Load in 3–8 minutes; inference at 3–10 tokens/sec
- After first download: model loads from cache in 5–30 seconds

## Privacy & Data Handling

- Model inference is 100% local — inputs and outputs never leave the device
- No user accounts, no login, no personal data collected
- Vercel Analytics collects anonymized pageview data only (URL, country, device type, referrer — no inference content)
- NEXT_LOCALE cookie stores language preference (1 year, no personal data)
- No advertising cookies, no tracking pixels, no third-party cookies
- Open source: verify at https://github.com/atameralican

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: What makes WebMind AI different from ChatGPT or Claude?**
A: ChatGPT and Claude send your prompts to OpenAI/Anthropic servers and require accounts. WebMind AI runs entirely in your browser — your prompts never leave your device, no account is needed, and it's free and open source.

**Q: How is WebMind AI different from Ollama or LM Studio?**
A: Ollama and LM Studio require installation on your computer and admin privileges. WebMind AI runs in a browser tab — no installation, no admin rights, works on any shared computer or school laptop.

**Q: Does WebMind AI work offline?**
A: Yes, after the initial model download. Models are cached in your browser's storage. Once downloaded, you can disconnect from the internet and continue using the model.

**Q: Which models are best for privacy-sensitive documents?**
A: All WebMind AI models are equally private — none send data to servers. For sensitive work, Phi-3.5 Mini (MIT license, 2.2 GB) or Llama 3.2 3B (1.9 GB) offer good quality at manageable sizes.

**Q: Can Transformers.js run on Firefox or Safari?**
A: Yes. Transformers.js uses ONNX Runtime with WebAssembly fallback, which works on all modern browsers. WebGPU acceleration is Chrome/Edge only, but CPU inference runs on Firefox and Safari.

**Q: What is the smallest model I can run?**
A: The smallest model is DistilBERT Sentiment at 67 MB (text classification only). For LLM chat, Llama 3.2 1B at 800 MB is the smallest option and runs on devices with 1 GB VRAM.

**Q: Is WebMind AI HIPAA compliant?**
A: WebMind AI's architecture (100% local inference, no cloud transmission) is favorable for HIPAA scenarios because PHI never leaves the device. However, WebMind AI itself is not a HIPAA Business Associate and provides no BAA. Compliance depends on your full system configuration.

## Use Cases

- **Healthcare**: Summarize clinical notes without PHI leaving the workstation
- **Legal**: Analyze documents without waiving attorney-client privilege to cloud services
- **Education**: Students can use AI on school computers without creating accounts
- **Developers**: Build browser-native AI features without API keys or inference costs
- **Enterprise**: Evaluate LLMs without sending proprietary data to third-party APIs

## Key Facts

- License: MIT (open source)
- Languages: English, Turkish (auto-detection via Accept-Language header)
- Creator: Alican Atamer
- GitHub: https://github.com/atameralican
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/alican-atamer/
- Built with: Next.js 16, WebGPU API, MLC AI WebLLM, Hugging Face Transformers.js, Tailwind CSS v4, next-intl
